excerpt: Quantum Chess — March 2025 Members of the Clemson Quantum club gathered for a game of Quantum Chess, exploring the intersection of classical strategy and quantum mechanics. Players made moves governed by quantum rules such as superposition and entanglement, leading to surprising and educational game

title: 2025 CUHackIt
excerpt: 2025 CUHackIt CUHackIt is Clemson University's annual student hackathon. In 2025, the Clemson Quantum Club participated with quantum computing workshops and mentoring. Highlights - Quantum Workshop: Introduction to quantum gates and circuits using Qiskit - Team Challenges: Hands-on quantum algorithm

title: Clemson teams sweep SC Quantathon, win trip to international competition
excerpt: Two Clemson teams placed at the SC Quantathon and secured an invitation to compete internationally. Their projects focused on practical quantum algorithms and hardware-aware optimizations. Read the full Clemson News story for details and photos. For the original article, see the Clemson News link ab

title: Students hack the future — one qubit at a time
excerpt: Students from across campus participated in a quantum-focused hackathon that included tutorials, hands-on demos, and teams building small quantum prototypes and simulators. The event highlighted student innovation in quantum computing and outreach. See the full Clemson News article via the link abov

title: Clemson team goes global after SC Quantathon win
excerpt: A Clemson team captured first place at the SC Quantathon and received an invitation to present their work at an international forum. Their project demonstrated novel approaches to quantum error mitigation and benchmarking. Read more on the SC Quantum website.

title: Detecting Majorana Zero Mode with ResNet and Winding Number Invariant
excerpt: Abstract We train a ResNet on simulated conductance/wavefunction data to classify the presence of Majorana zero modes and compare classifier outputs to winding-number-based invariants. Results indicate complementary strengths for data-driven and analytic approaches. Authors & affiliations - Ian Lewi

title: Hardware-Aware Quantum Circuit Synthesis
excerpt: Abstract This project explores compilation and synthesis techniques that produce circuits tailored to a target device's native gates and topology, improving practical execution fidelity. Authors & affiliations - Toby Cox — Clemson University Creative Inquiry - Ian Lewis — Clemson University Creative

title: Advancing Materials Development with Quantum AI and VQE Simulations of Majorana Edge States and Topological Phases
excerpt: Abstract This poster presents work combining VQE simulations and machine learning techniques to probe Majorana edge states and topological phases relevant to materials design. The project demonstrates how quantum simulations can support identification of topological signatures and potential material
